reviews.review_text:
i got this game on sale for around 3$ and it blew me away. The graphics, visuals and the whole experience were better than I expected. It's crazy to think this game came out in 2013.
hear me out, after playing this masterpiece I'm a huge Tomb Raider fan now.

excellent simulator of killing an infinite amount of men on an island.

the game is insanely easy even on hard mode and the story is super basic and cliche - but somehow i’ve beaten it 3 times already. good reboot for Lara Croft

super smooth onboarding, amazing level design, sound design and weapon progression. the puzzles and tombs are interesting too.

horrible QTEs, who the hell puts the hotkey at the bottom of the screen while the actual action is happening in the middle?? still love this game anyway

We all wish that we could forget about the game just to play it again. Well for me this game was just that. I had completely forgotten about the story and the combat system and how absolute distructive this game was. From the stealth, to the hand to hand, to the guns we have. Obviously i knew about the bow and the climbing/parkour. AND OBVIOUSLY the character herself. 

For a 2013 game graphic still holds but its not perfect according to todays standard. very limited gameplay control customization. That camera constantly shaking was so bad. Thankfull didnt got motion sickness. Also no sprint?  

Beside that It was amazing. I breached through the story just as a rewind but the story i would say is just "good" in the context of the game. The real game lies in everything else. She got some insane luck, strenght and durabitly lol.

4.5/5

I finally got around to playing this game, and I was honestly surprised by how much I enjoyed it. I was expecting it to be of a lower quality - given it is now 13 years old!

Even though the game is that old, it still looks great and has a really immersive atmosphere. There were plenty of moments that had me hooked and wanting to see what happened next.

Overall, I had a great time with it and can definitely see why so many people enjoyed it too. If you're into the other Tomb Raider games, I'd say it's well worth playing. It was probably my favourite - after Rise of the Tomb Raider.
Ended up buying the bundle. I thought this game would feel outdated but turns out its still great

Had this game for ages and never played it consistently. It show its age but runs great on the steam deck, so perfect for portable fun.

The game is a bit stiff; but in its prime it was quite a marvel to see. Graphics still holds up but it is very confusing due to the textures blurring and environment not having modern lighting rendering, so everything is kinda mashed up together and hard to see unless you play on a large monitor. 

Combat is fun; very similar to Uncharted games with usual tropes and things to activate to unlock areas. It has a bit more freedom than Uncharted games as it is less linear (don't recall the first 3 Uncharted games having save points used as fast travel on the map). As such you can go back and find stuff if you missed it, but it is more for show than anything as the game does not require you to do that to complete the story.

In 2026 it is still a 7 out of 10 for me; looking forward to play the other 2 games in this series.

This game has great atmosphere and really captures the "stranded on an island" isolation feel. Lara Croft is a well developed and likeable character; also very sharp with a bow and arrow. On the downside, the secondary characters are poor and shallow, which leaves Lara with a heavy load to carry on her back (which she does quite well). The story itself is well paced but isn't anything special.
Overall Tomb Raider is strong in some points but weak in others, they did, however, nail the action/adventure feel and set up the sequel with a solid foundation.
Holy amazing gameee... The story from starting is emotional, gave me literal goosebumps.. The story and the graphics are all top notch!! HEAVILY RECOMMENDED
Ringan banget buat spek laptopku, recomended gamenya story asik dan gameplay kerenn

good gameplay with super action cinematic events and okay story, but world looks not so good, the colors sucks!!, music not good too. but overall for the price when discounted is very good game except the constant camera shakings are insane. luckily the mod called "Disable Camera Wobble" saved the game fun or i would have not been able to finish it with constant motion sickness..
Still the best Lara Croft design from the series. The downside is that I couldn't last 5 minutes playing the game.

this game is absolutely insane
mega super unreal experience
totally crazy gameplay
10/10 no questions asked

Finally! I know this isnt the OG Tomb Raider but i can finally say i have played a TR game.  I grew up hearing about this franchise so I decided to buy this game on sale.  Still holds up 13 years later. I might even prefer it over Uncharted 1.  Cant wait to see what the other games bring!

Imagine the most bland and generic action film you have ever seen. Add Lara Croft as the main character. And there you have it, Tomb Raider 2013. 

The game has barely any puzzles (the main game has like five or six in 8 hours, and they are very easy). They are also present in dlc, but I encountered a bug in the very first one and didn't bother with the others.

The main gameplay is 50/50 light parkour and gunfights. Parkour is very basic, not even Assassin's Creed level. The gunfight is boring. You are mowing down dozens of generic dudes that are getting fatter as the game progresses. 

The story is weak. You got caught in a storm, and your friend got kidnapped. All the deaths are highly predictable, conflict inside of the crew makes no sense. No character growth outside of the realization that "Yeah, Dad was right. The world is full of mysterious things." Cinematics don't match gameplay. You are the John Wick in gameplay, and in a cinematic you are forced to run from five dudes. You constantly have a perfect sight line on the big bad guy, and you never try to kill him. Except for one time at the end, where during the harsh wind she decides to shoot him with a bow instead of a gun, so smart!

I did enjoy the first five hours, but then it became a slog as it became a constant gunfight with dudes who can tank a couple point-blank slug shots. The negative review is only because I expected more from the Lara Croft franchise, if it were an original IP, I wouldn't bother with the game.

It's worth it, the gameplay is roughly 10 hrs long with full of action.  
It's a good game to start lara simping journey and rest is upto you.
Lara 10/10
parkour 10/10
combat 9/10
graphics 7/10
story 4/10
overall 7.5/10

Even 13 years later, this game still rocks!!  Played it in 2026 and enjoyed it a ton.  Great mix of platforming, exploration, puzzles, exploring, story, etc.  Loved the vibe and genre.  S tier for sure

A pretty solid reboot the gameplay is great. The combat feels good at range the melee is pretty poor, Looks really good for the age it was made,
But the story is a mess. at the start of the game she cold and hungry but never wears a jacket eats or needs to eat even tho the game tells you do some hunting for food really makes me think game was going be a stealth/survivor game then just when action later which seems to be a good call to do as its good game just story don't work in places well, 
The story she acts brave but deeply fearful of failing her group dying herself and being bad she upset with killing but the gameplay she really is like the Punisher It makes little sense to have both.  Quick-Time Events are just bad reminds me of RE ones, Its a really fun game gameplay wise just don't think to much on the story.
that`s a really good game, i didn`t have any expectative and this game surprise me a lot

Dont waste ur time on this game, its 2026 and its still full of bugs.
Most are not gamebreaking, enemies floating and bouncing etc.

But there are also bugs that make it so you cannot progress any further, and have to start over from scratch.
For me and many others ( see forum ) theres a box on a level called Geothermal caverns that doesnt go where it should and u have to replay the first 35% of the game to get back there, or download savefiles from some fan site to load into the game further ahead etc.

F U C K This game.
I really enjoyed hunting down all the collectibles after finishing the main story - the main story was awesome, by the way

I dont like the game at all even tho every one is glazing it (Spoilers)

So the story is meh everyone died bec of lara every thing she does is stupid and she have one of the most plot armor i have ever seen in my life there is no way out i cant do any thing suddenly a wind comes and wow there is a big plank shows up from no ware and she can escape now
there is alot of issues on the ui + the ui is horrible the 3d ui is one of the most horrible thing it is un usable
the game play was alright the puzzles were good the enemies were smart
but you can just skip them just spam shift and run you just skipped all of them
the saving system in this game was broken asf like i exit the game and reopen it it is not saved like 2-3h progress gone bec you had to sit on the fire place wow there is no auto saving in this game

A good game from the first Lara Croft series with puzzles, shootouts, and stealth.
Recommended.

DO NOT BUY THIS GAME!!
There is a massive camera shake/wobble that causes nausea and headaches for people and there is no option to turn it off.

This game is really nice and if it got a modern remaster it would be so much better.

The way Lara moves is super janky, which would not be that bad if it didn't have the bugs on top of it.

If you touch this game you WILL experience a bug or multiple bugs during your playthrough.

Phasing, soft-locking, those are so normal its insane, the game is 13 years old sure, but this is A LOT of problems during the playthrough making a nice game to play almost unbearable.

сюжет хороший, геймплей просто великолепен. ни разу за всю игру не было душно, играя на самой высокой сложности в большинстве случаев не чувствовал никакого сопротивления. постоянные стоны лары оцениваю на 10

80%
+ Great combat and traversal mechanics
+ Fantastic map design, variety and graphics
+ Interesting characters and environment
- Story and action sequences are often cliche
- Some of Lara's performance/overall dialogue is mediocre
- Too many enemies/large action sequences, needed more adventuring
Great gameplay, amazing sound, and just overall pretty. With one mayor flaw: Camera shake that makes me want to vomit after 5 min of playing. Can not even complete the game

I recently finished Tomb Raider (2013), and I absolutely loved it. The story was incredibly engaging, and the entire survival aspect of being stranded on an island while trying to save your friends kept me invested from start to finish.

The graphics are excellent, and it's hard to believe this game was released in 2013. The environments, weather effects, and overall visual quality still hold up remarkably well today.

The gameplay mechanics were also fantastic. The combat, exploration, and use of different tools felt fresh and exciting. One of my favorite parts was the escape sequences, where the environment around you collapses and breaks apart. Those moments were intense and made me feel like I was part of an action movie.

I especially enjoyed the dodge-and-attack combat mechanics. The final boss fight was one of the highlights of the game, requiring constant dodging and attacking through multiple phases, which made it both challenging and satisfying.

This was also the first game where I used a bow extensively, and I found it much more fun than I expected. Landing shots and taking down enemies with the bow was incredibly satisfying.

The final moments of the game felt like something straight out of a movie and provided a great conclusion to Lara's journey. Overall, Tomb Raider (2013) was an amazing experience, and I would highly recommend it to anyone who enjoys action-adventure games with a strong story, great gameplay, and memorable characters.

Tomb Raider is honestly one of the best games I’ve ever played. The exploration is incredibly fun and rewarding. Every area feels worth searching, and the collectibles never become tedious or feel like pointless filler. Finding hidden secrets, documents, relics, and upgrades always felt exciting. The optional tombs are another highlight of the game. They feature clever puzzles that make you think without becoming frustratingly difficult. Solving them is satisfying, and they add a lot to the sense of adventure. While a few of the challenges near the end of the game can feel a little tedious, that’s a very minor issue in an otherwise fantastic experience. The gameplay is smooth, responsive, and consistently enjoyable from beginning to end. One thing I love is how the game avoids becoming repetitive. You’re constantly unlocking new tools, weapons, abilities, and skills that keep exploration and combat feeling fresh. The pacing is nearly perfect, with the game steadily introducing new mechanics and challenges at just the right moments. The story and characters are also excellent. Lara Croft’s journey from an inexperienced survivor to a capable adventurer is compelling and believable. The voice acting is outstanding, especially Lara’s performance, which brings so much emotion and authenticity to the character. The collectible notes and journals are another great touch, adding depth to the island’s history, lore, and supporting characters, making the world feel much richer and more interesting. Overall, Tomb Raider is a masterpiece and one of the greatest action adventure games ever made. It delivers fantastic exploration, satisfying gameplay, memorable characters, and an engaging story. I genuinely believe it’s a game that everyone should play at least once in their lifetime. It’s that good. I also hope that future Tomb Raider remakes and games take inspiration from this masterpiece, because it remains one of the finest examples of what the franchise can achieve. 10/10

the game is great i really enjoyed playing it and dicovering the story. I have no comments other that i wish it was more action packed with harder combat, but at teh end of the day its more of an adventure kind of game.
Solid game, longer than I anticipated. Combat is fun and the upgrades you acquire are satisfying. Heavy emphasis on exploration so check every nook and cranny. Laura Croft is a star per usual!

Great 10-ish hour action story and it certainly doesn't hurt that Lara Croft is very hot. 
On the topic of hurting, she does get hurt A LOT, so if that entices you (which, in playing the game, I worryingly discovered it did for me) then I strongly recommend it.

Honestly did not expect this game to hit as hard as it did. The story pulls you in right from the start and Lara actually feels like a real character going through some rough ♥♥♥♥. Combat is satisfying, the exploration is fun, puzzles are not too hard not too easy. Environments look gorgeous too.
The game is fun. I love the death cutscenes. They're brutal
Pretty fun and now for the sale they are doing it's def giving it a try if youre into games like resident evil
After so many years I finally played this game again, she's actually my game crush besides Ada Wong 🖤
best action adventure i ever played. in my opinion its better than uncharted. and this game is absolutely worth playing. A MUST TRY!!!
I have playes many trips-A games but personally I loved this game, bought it for like 70 inr or something. I would tell its worth it for real man. Loved it too much

Pretty worth it game. Forgiving as well and a lot of fun to play.

Highly Recommend

This is my first time playing any tomb raider game and I've been playing almost every single day since I bought this game. I got the Lara Croft fortnite skin and watched the 2018 film years prior and I gotta say this game has been a breathe of fresh air for me from playing the same few repetitive online multiplayer game.
Sometimes crashes when i want to open the game but overall great.

This game is just as fun as when it was released but the bugs you will run into on this game will have you frustrated. Most of the bugs I have ran into i either had to return to main menu and reload save or die. Unfortunately I have  now run into a bug that is preventing me from finishing the game. Luckily I just had to tweak some settings to get it to work. Overall it's a really fun game if you don't let the bugs get to you.

Tomb Raider (2013) is, in my opinion, a fun game that is a bit heavy on the cut-scenes. This is my first tomb raider game so I don’t know the status quo, however, I really enjoyed this game. I like the combat, the parkour, and the exploration was really enjoyable. I especially enjoyed the gun play for how simple yet fun it was. The story was also really good if a bit simple but it was a good enough excuse to parkour. The only thing I wasn’t a big fan of was the cut scenes, because there was a lot of them, especially at the beginning of the game. I will say, though they started becoming a few and far between as the game continued. As for the steam deck performance, it was amazing. I played everything at normal settings, which you could bump it up higher and still get a smooth 60 FPS. However, there was a boss fight that needed to be played at 30 FPS, but that was the only major bug I could find. Overall, the game was really fun and the characters (especially Lara Croft) were really fun, even if the side characters didn’t appear much. The game is definitely worth it on sale, but even full price I would say it’s not bad. 8/10 “I hate tombs.”
good fun and cheap game when you buy this i recoomend to get the trilogy this game gives atleast 15+ hours if you get all colecteables, sadly the mutiplayer has a 30 minute que time for one game
This was one of the most cinematic gaming experiences I've had. I can't get over just how beautiful this game looks, and how smooth it runs; absolutely worth buying, highly recommend it.
Great storyline, brilliant locations - very playable. A new twist on a classic game.

My first Tomb Raider game, and it definitely made me interested in the rest of the series.

Great story, fun combat, satisfying exploration, and a progression system that doesn't make you grind for hours just to unlock something useful. Everything felt well-paced and rewarding.

An epic action-adventure experience from beginning to end.
And yes, being able to spend the whole game looking at Lara Croft is a nice bonus
I'm late to the party, but this was a solid showing for Lara and a pretty great Tomb Raider game.
I never could have guessed that this game could be this good, packed with action it s such a beautifull story, it needs a remaster badly!
The story's amazing nd the gameplay is sick.
Addictive. For what now we call an "old" game, this is a piece of great craftmanship. Combat is fun and i just love looting and solving puzzles.

A really good game , game graphics are very good according to 2013 and still feels relevent and game is full of chaos a decent stroy game dips for some time like 40 min only in between but the rest of game was brilliant and if you are planing to play this game so pls don't forget to play with a controller

I just finished *Tomb Raider (2013)* and I honestly had a great time with it. It feels like a really good Lara Croft adventure, with a nice mix of action, exploration, and tomb raiding. The game keeps things exciting throughout, and I enjoyed discovering new areas and taking on the different challenges along the way.

The storyline was solid and kept me interested from beginning to end. Watching Lara grow and become the survivor we know was one of my favorite parts of the experience.

I was also surprised by how good the graphics still look. For a game that came out in 2013, the environments, character models, and overall presentation are still very impressive.

Overall, I highly recommend *Tomb Raider (2013)*, especially if you're a Lara Croft fan. It's an enjoyable adventure with great action, memorable moments, and plenty of tomb exploration. Definitely worth playing!

When I first jumped into Tomb Raider, I wasn’t sure how much I’d connect with Lara Croft’s origin story. But honestly, the game surprised me. It’s not just about raiding tombs — it’s about survival, exploration, and watching Lara grow from a vulnerable young adventurer into a hardened survivor.

The gameplay mixes action, stealth, and puzzle‑solving really well. I loved how the environments felt alive — climbing cliffs, sneaking through forests, and discovering hidden relics kept me hooked. The combat can feel intense at times, especially when you’re outnumbered, but the bow quickly became my favorite weapon.

If your screen is cut off, go into properties and under betas downgrade to build 743.  If you end up in a room that you cant get out of and see a door thats almost halfway open, reload your save.

Decided to playthrough this since i picked the rest of the trilogy up.  Game holds up well and was fun all the way through.  There is some jank with the parkour and platforming in later levels, but thoroughly enjoyed it.

Tomb Raider (2013) isn't just a reboot; it’s a masterclass in character-driven action-adventure. It successfully stripped away the campy, untouchable hero persona of the original Lara Croft and replaced her with someone vulnerable, gritty, and fiercely resilient.

If you appreciate tight pacing, cinematic presentation, and a story that actually makes you feel the stakes, this is a must-play.

Whether you are a fan of the classic series or a newcomer looking for a top-tier action game, Tomb Raider (2013) delivers on almost every front. It set the bar high for what a modern reboot should be.
I love this game. hyperinteractive, hot characters, and nice plot.

This was my first Tomb Raider, and it feels like I'm playing it for the first time again. Beautiful graphics and landscape, deeply unsettling location, and challenging yet fruitful gameplay. A little buggy here and there, but doesn't ruin experience.

Tomb Raider was never my cup of tea but... oh my ! this one is pretty amazing. Even though I started playing this game in 2026, it was a great experience. Graphics hold up great for the age of the game, gameplay is smooth. I don't often finish my games but now I want to finish all the Tomb raiders. I'm a fan now.

Enjoyability - 10/10
Soundtrack - 8/10
Mod Content - 4/10
Difficulty - 7/10
Story - 10/10

In my opinion this is the best game out of the whole survivor trilogy.

I bought this game in January and have started a new save file 3 times (only 1 of which I have finished). After finishing the game yesterday, I am giving it a 7.2/10. It was pretty good and worth the price. The story was pretty cliche (I was highkey invested though) and the gameplay was janky at times, but it didn't take away from the experience. It was fun looking around the place for collectibles and doing the puzzles in each of the tombs. The game was pretty easy even on the hardest difficulty. The only times I died was when I was being stupid or I messed up on the parkour. The story was very entertaining and the characters were very likeable. Overall I would recommend this game.

pretty mediocre, fun gameplay yeah sure but other than the game is very mediocre in game design bad story bad characters and a lot of times you will ask  " WHY IS LARA LOBOTOMIZED WHY ISNT SHE TAKING THE SHOT WHEN SHE CAN SHE IS ALREADY A MASS MURDERER". its an okay action game to play when you have literallly nothing else to do.
SO cool. Too many bugss thoo. But other than that very cool story and plot but maybe a tad bit too many fight scenes (im not complaining) but other than that loved everythingg
Nice story nice graphics nice everything!

For some reason I was expecting this to be more like Metro Exodus, but its more like Uncharted, which is fine but the action combat was not so polished to make up most of the gameplay loop I feel.

Overall it was okay. I would have 100% it bc the tombs were fun, but the multiplayer achievements kind of ruined that for me. Also a shame they pussied out on making her gay, but maybe the'll fix that later

(30 of my hours are afk due to when I close the game its still "running" and i forget to turn it off) 
Real fun so far ima try and get all achivements till I actually give a more proper review but yeah, its been great.
Does what an origin story should. Combat feels good, the island looks great, puzzles are easy but not annoying. Wanted to 100% it but multiplayer is dead so that's cooked.

I've refunded. 
I remember this game pretty fondlly, played the game some years ago and enjoyed it. Enough to buy it again on sale. But so many people complain about gamebreaking bugs and the game softlocking the game that I got a bit scared (Especially after battling so much with Far Cry 3). For me, the game already had issues right at the start with descriptions being bugged out the window and there being no fix for that. And since others complained about that aswell I just have to assume that I would encounter all the other issues aswell. SAD!
Maybe some day others will find workarounds or (imagine that) the company behind this game comes back and fixes it themselves. Until then: Do not buy this. Even if it's under 3€. If it's broken, don't buy it.
I absolutely loved everything about this game. The story is great, and upgrading the weapons over time adds a rewarding sense of progression. I only wish it hadn't ended.

Tomb Raider is an amazing reboot that made Lara Croft feel more human and relatable.

Watching Lara grow from a scared survivor into a fearless adventurer is what makes the story so engaging. The game balances exploration, combat, platforming, and survival elements really well, so it never feels boring.

The environments look beautiful, especially the ruins, forests, and dangerous mountain areas. Climbing, solving puzzles, and discovering hidden tombs gives a real sense of adventure.

The combat is also surprisingly fun, with the bow easily becoming one of the most satisfying weapons in the game.

Action-packed, emotional, and cinematic from start to finish.

A fantastic modern adventure game.
amazing game, great mix of parkour and gunplay.

I was having so much fun for the first two hours but I unfortunately had to refund. It continued to crash every time I died, leading it to be completely unplayable. The UI is out of the screen and it cannot be fixed no matter the settings I changed the game to. Big bummer.

Wasn't actually expecting much from a game released a decade ago, bought this just for its name and it's actually the first I played from the series as it's been heavily recommended as well. After playing for hours completing the storyline, I could confidently say I loved this game! From the graphics to its gameplay, it's pretty much straight forward.
combat and movement is sooo fluid its like the last of us part 2 but better and the story isn't poo
Amazing graphics and gameplay.
good game havent played seires sense original but good modern game for what it is

amazing just amazing, bought this game cuz it was on sale and i did not expect myself to actually enjoy playing it this much, personally the combination of me being able to explore, solve puzzle and engage in lot of combat is perfect for me, i think i just discover what kind of gameplay i enjoy the most.

O primeiro jogo da trilogia começada em 2011, é também considerado um dos melhores. Apesar da época lançado, o jogo continua com gráficos bonitos nos dias atuais (jogando com os gráficos no ultra). A história é muito boa, e a gameplay prende o jogador do início ao fim.

你敢信这是和生化危机6同年出的？？？因为生9才克服心理障碍重新尝试打游戏，这游戏体验强好多，解密跑酷收集piupiu都有，目前感受是各方面平衡得不错。对劳拉的塑造及其他人的互作都非常细节，光是从杀第一只鹿到第一个人的刻画完全超越了RE2里昂淡淡然几句wtf ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥，主角什么样你自己僵硬地想象吧(不然你就做成林克那种无性格哑巴)。。。。或许是不同风格吧，不过古墓9太细了连文献都有配音，收集物也是合理嵌入设定的，喜
for a game that came out over a decade ago the game still looks grate playes well and the story is like uncharted. the menus give me seizures tho

I finished Uncharted 4 and needed a game like it. I thought the Tomb Raider series might be the case and It was on sale.

After Completion, I can say its short but sweet. Worth it when on sale. Has good set pieces and constant action. The ending is top tier. It starts off slow intentionally but then throws you into the action. Reminds me of a 90's action movie. Good story, Excellent Graphics, Good Gameplay (once you get used to it). Only let down is some bugs, (especially the v-sync one). Other than that 9/10.
The main story of the game might not be it's strongest point but it sure is hella fun to play the game,definitely one of the best in the trilogy
Its a fun game with good mechanics and a great story.

It's a good game but at the start without weapon upgrades you are weak I recommend saving your salvage for the handgun upgrades and using skill points wisely some annoying parts but great to pick up on sale

I had been meaning to play this game ever since it came out in 2013. I am glad I took the time to play it now on modern hardware. It runs so smoothly and looks beautiful. I am sure part of that is due to the technical upgrade the game got during the PS4 generation. The gunplay is satisfying. The platforming is tight and responsive. Don't skip the optional tombs. Each one is a short platforming puzzle and they were probably my favorite part. I was craving a short cinematic action game and this hit the spot. Looking forward to playing the sequels at some point.
the gameplay is occasionally buggy, but the overall story and playthrough is fun. Rare that a game like this is playable on a macbook

I played Tomb Raider entirely on the Steam Deck, and it was a fantastic experience. The game runs very smoothly, looks great even by today's standards, and the controls feel perfectly suited for handheld play.

The mix of action, exploration, platforming, and light puzzle-solving keeps the gameplay engaging throughout. Lara Croft's origin story is well told, and her character development from a survivor into a confident adventurer is one of the game's highlights.

Even more than a decade after its release, Tomb Raider remains an excellent action-adventure game. On the Steam Deck, it's an easy recommendation and a lot of fun from start to finish.

8.5/10.

I really enjoyed playing through this game.
I love the setting of the island, I loved the characters and how well they were written and acted.
The lore was really nice, I felt like these could all be real people and a reasonably real story. Obviously some fantasy thrown in but that's okay. 
Unfortunately since this game is 13 years old, I am unable to play the multiplayer so I am not sure about that.
I think the skill tree and upgrades were nice. Nothing crazy, pretty simply but it didn't need to be anything crazy.
The puzzles and collectibles were pretty intuitive and simple. I had to look up some things but that's just because I'm a little slow.
and ~15 hours to fully 100% is pretty awesome.
Makes me very excited to play the next two games, hoping they're just as good.
4/5
I've always loved this game. My issue is I completed everything in game and i didn't get some achievements I should have, and it's stuck at 99% completion.

Its crazy that this was Lara's first mission and everything possible went wrong. You can slowly see her start to accept that something supernatural is going on and you can see her leave her old self behind. The visuals in this game was def something worth to see and the action was really good to but I didn't think I was capable of getting lasered by NPCs when I would try to transition between cover lol.

-i played this game in my childhood.
-really enjoyed it
-see on steam 
-bought it to play after 12 years
-still one of the most fun and enjoyable game with great graphic despite being 13 years old game
-i wish other game studios take example from this game
-10/10 game for me  :)

Tomb Raider is easily one of the absolute best games in its genre, and there is zero debate about it. It is honestly hilarious seeing people complain about the visuals. Despite its age, it delivers a far better story and experience than the average garbage released in 2026.

Right from the start, the menu would inexplicably scroll so fast, it was almost impossible to make a selection. Then, 4 hours into the game, the character began to autorun. I tried new keyboard, reinstalling the game, internet searches, contacting Crytal Dynamics, and couldnt find a fix. It's too bad, the game was fun
This game is amazing. It still looks incredibly good for its age, and the gameplay is surprisingly satisfying. Definitely worth playing.

While some of the climbing and mechanics are clunky due to its age, the game was fun.  The combat was solid, and I liked the progression of the moves.  The story was good, and allows you to be on or off task.

This game is value for money. Don't expect many hour content cause the game is old. One thing i don't like is some achievement need multiplayer but people are not playing the game multiplayer does not work. This game will work in any pc

Played this on the Xbox 360 a long time ago when I was a kid. Then I decided to replay this on steam now that I am older. This game is awesome. Gameplay is fun. Lara Croft is lovely to stare at. Story is nice, I love how interactive the cutscenes will get, very immersive.

Good Game.

Still holds up Incredibly well, both graphically and gameplay wise. Few bugs here and there. And it sucks there's so many multiplayer tied achievements that will be nightmare for 100% completionists to do since no one plays it. But a really fun single player campaign all around. The combat and gunplay is quite addicting.

Tomb Raider is an exceptionally satisfying origin story for Lara Croft. Following her journey from an inspired and naive explorer to a hardened, resourceful adventurer is a rewarding experience.

Platforming, acrobatics, exploration and a combat system that can go into close and personal melee, use a hail of fire from shotguns, rifles and even grenades to a stealth approach with quiet headshots, and nimble takedowns.

Its Game of the year for a reason. Best start to a new Tomb Raider timeline. SImple and enjoyable on every aspect such as combat, parkouring, exploration, collectible lore and characters. Very Dense and enjoyable package. Shocked i completed everything in main story in 18 hours. 

10/10 medium length game fun on multiple aspects

PS : Extra Star for being so optimised i can play on medium on a work laptop.
